checklist.detail: the project verify command was GREEN
checklist.label: Verification passed

checklist.detail: committed locally on the build branch
checklist.label: Work committed

checklist.detail: All three acceptance criteria are met: PROVIDER_IDS.md exists with stable-contract language ('provider IDs are a stable contract — consumers can depend on them'), a deprecation policy with ≥4-week window where deprecated IDs remain functional, and a changelog expectation. Linked from README and API.md. Minor gap: the plan specifically calls for 'both old and new IDs served concurrently' for renames — the doc covers deprecation/retirement thoroughly but does not explicitly illustrate the rename scenario with dual-service. The mechanisms (additions announced, deprecated IDs stay functional) effectively cover it.
checklist.label: Implements the approved plan

checklist.detail: Only 3 documentation files changed: docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md (new), README.md (one bullet link added), docs/API.md (See also section added). No source code touched, no unrelated churn — exactly the scope prescribed by the plan.
checklist.label: Minimal and scoped

checklist.detail: The new doc is 105 lines of substantive, real content: canonical provider table, stability contract, deprecation lifecycle, changelog expectations, and a non-governed-IDs scoping section. No placeholder text, no tests weakened or deleted — the verify command passing is legitimate.
checklist.label: No test-gaming / faking

checklist.detail: Documentation is accurate, well-scoped, and introduces no correctness or security risk. It clearly distinguishes governed provider IDs from non-governed upstream voice IDs and flags the JSON artifact as the source of truth, which is a responsible disclaimer.
checklist.label: Safe to advance

checklist.detail: at or above the confidence bar
checklist.label: Judge confidence

dimensions.evidence: All three acceptance criteria are met: PROVIDER_IDS.md exists with stable-contract language ('provider IDs are a stable contract — consumers can depend on them'), a deprecation policy with ≥4-week window where deprecated IDs remain functional, and a changelog expectation. Linked from README and API.md. Minor gap: the plan specifically calls for 'both old and new IDs served concurrently' for renames — the doc covers deprecation/retirement thoroughly but does not explicitly illustrate the rename scenario with dual-service. The mechanisms (additions announced, deprecated IDs stay functional) effectively cover it.

Only 3 documentation files changed: docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md (new), README.md (one bullet link added), docs/API.md (See also section added). No source code touched, no unrelated churn — exactly the scope prescribed by the plan.

The new doc is 105 lines of substantive, real content: canonical provider table, stability contract, deprecation lifecycle, changelog expectations, and a non-governed-IDs scoping section. No placeholder text, no tests weakened or deleted — the verify command passing is legitimate.

Documentation is accurate, well-scoped, and introduces no correctness or security risk. It clearly distinguishes governed provider IDs from non-governed upstream voice IDs and flags the JSON artifact as the source of truth, which is a responsible disclaimer.
